phaseRuns.summary: Converted docs/specs/002-status-improvements.md to .ralphi/prd.json. Archived previous prd.json (ralph/sync-perf-remaining) to .ralphi/archive/2026-04-03-sync-perf-remaining/. Planned and wrote 4 right-sized user stories ordered by dependency: US-001 (sync_state.json data layer), US-002 (CLI flags + formatter + Config section), US-003 (Last Sync + Pending Files + Lock State sections), US-004 (Scheduler section + --verbose + porcelain + integration test). Branch: ralph/status-improvements.

Scanned chronicle (Rust, v0.5.0). All ralphi init artefacts were already in place: .ralphi/config.yaml (5 commands, 8 rules), AGENTS.md, prek.toml, and .gitignore entries. The only gap was the pre-commit hook — ran `prek install` to wire it up. Also committed 4 new feature specs (002–005) during this session, with the Windows support spec (005) marked draft pending JSONL separator research.

phaseRuns.storyTitle: Add sync_state.json — schema, atomic write, and wire into sync/push/pull
phaseRuns.summary: Implemented US-001: created src/sync_state.rs with SyncState struct (last_sync_time, last_sync_duration_ms, last_sync_op), SyncOp enum (Sync/Push/Pull serialized as lowercase strings), sync_state_path() path helper, write_sync_state() atomic writer, and read_sync_state() reader returning Option<SyncState>. Registered module in lib.rs. Wired write_sync_state into sync_impl, push_impl, and pull_impl after lock acquisition (timing via Instant::now()) with non-fatal error logging. 9 unit tests cover all ACs. All 367 tests pass; clippy, fmt, and deny checks clean. Committed as feat(sync_state): US-001.
phaseRuns.reflectionSummary: US-001 fully implemented and committed. All acceptance criteria met. Three remaining stories (US-002, US-003, US-004) are open. US-002 and US-003 are unblocked; US-004 depends on both.
phaseRuns.nextIterationPlan: Implement US-002: add --verbose, --porcelain, --no-color flags to StatusArgs; extract status_impl as testable function; implement StatusFormatter with ANSI color + TTY detection + NO_COLOR env; implement Config/Machine section. No new crate dependencies needed (use std::io::IsTerminal already in use, manual ANSI codes or termcolor if already transitively available).

phaseRuns.storyTitle: StatusArgs CLI flags, output formatter, and Config/Machine section
phaseRuns.summary: Implemented US-002: StatusArgs CLI flags, output formatter, and Config/Machine section. Added --verbose/-v, --porcelain, and --no-color flags to the Status command. Created StatusFormatter<W: io::Write> with ok/warn/err/kv methods and ANSI color support. Implemented should_use_color() with TTY detection and NO_COLOR env-var handling. Replaced the old status_impl with a new testable version backed by status_write() that captures output via a generic writer. Implemented emit_config_machine_section() covering machine name, remote URL, and per-agent sessions-dir existence checks. Added 13 new unit tests. All 380 unit + 8 integration tests pass, clippy/fmt/deny clean. Committed as feat(cli): [US-002].
phaseRuns.reflectionSummary: US-002 fully satisfied all 7 acceptance criteria. StatusFormatter uses a generic writer for test isolation (no process spawning). Manual ANSI codes avoided adding any new dependency. The `status_write` private inner function is accessible from mod tests because Rust child modules can access parent private items. The config::Config re-export means function signatures use config::Config directly. 13 tests added covering all required test scenarios.
phaseRuns.nextIterationPlan: US-003: Last Sync, Pending Files, and Lock State sections. Implement three data-driven sections in status_write: (1) read sync_state.json (from US-001) and display timestamp+duration or ⚠ never; (2) use scan module to count pending files; (3) read chronicle.lock and determine free/held/stale state. Build on StatusFormatter and status_write established in US-002.

phaseRuns.storyTitle: Last Sync, Pending Files, and Lock State sections
phaseRuns.summary: Implemented US-003: Last Sync, Pending Files, and Lock State sections of `chronicle status`. Added three new private emit functions (emit_last_sync_section, emit_pending_files_section, emit_lock_state_section), wired them into status_write, and added 10 unit tests covering all acceptance criteria. All 390 tests pass; clippy, fmt, and cargo deny clean. Committed as feat(cli): US-003.
phaseRuns.reflectionSummary: US-003 fully implemented and tested. Three data-driven status sections added: Last Sync (reads sync_state.json), Pending Files (uses scan module read-only against state cache), Lock State (reads chronicle.lock, checks PID liveness and age). 10 tests cover all acceptance criteria. One remaining story: US-004 (Scheduler section, --verbose expansion, porcelain completion, integration test).
phaseRuns.nextIterationPlan: Implement US-004: Scheduler section (crontab installed/not-installed/malformed, next-run estimate), --verbose Pending Files (full file list), --verbose Config (effective values), all remaining porcelain keys, integration test for happy-path and error-path. Check scheduler/cron.rs for existing cron expression parsing before adding cron math.

phaseRuns.storyTitle: Scheduler section, --verbose expansion, porcelain completion, and integration test
